Name: CERT
LongName: Consensus on Exercise Reporting Template (CERT): Explanation and Elaboration Statement
Description: CERT is a 16-item exercise-intervention reporting checklist (with sub-items) to improve completeness, reproducibility, and implementation usefulness of reported exercise programs.
Aim: To improve how exercise interventions are reported by ensuring clear detail on equipment, provider qualifications, delivery, progression, tailoring, dosage, setting, and adherence/fidelity.

Scope.Coverage: Manuscripts that evaluate or describe exercise interventions, including clinical and public-health exercise programs delivered in supervised, unsupervised, individual, group, or home-based settings.
Scope.Focus: Detailed exercise intervention reporting: equipment, provider qualifications, delivery mode, adherence measurement, motivation strategies, progression rules, exercise content, home components, non-exercise components, adverse events, dosage, tailoring, starting level, and planned/actual fidelity.
Scope.ContrastWith: CERT is a method-specific reporting overlay for exercise interventions and should be used alongside the primary design guideline for the study type (for example CONSORT for randomized trials).

primary.description: Slade SC, Dionne CE, Underwood M, Buchbinder R. Consensus on Exercise Reporting Template (CERT): Explanation and Elaboration Statement. Br J Sports Med. 2016;50(23):1428-1437.

items.item_question: Is the type of exercise equipment described in detail?
items.item_question_background: Equipment details are required to reproduce exercise exposure.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract all equipment used (for example weights, machines, treadmill, cycle ergometer).

source_page_refs.quote: Detailed description of the type of exercise equipment...

items.item_question_background: Provider competence can influence intervention fidelity and outcomes.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract exercise provider qualifications, supervisory expertise, and training undertaken.

source_page_refs.quote: Detailed description of the qualifications... training undertaken by the exercise instructor

items.item_question: Is it stated whether exercises are performed individually or in groups?
items.item_question_background: Delivery format changes feasibility, adherence, and effect interpretation.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract whether intervention is individual, group, or mixed format.

source_page_refs.quote: Describe whether exercises are performed individually or in a group

items.item_question: Is supervision status and delivery method described?
items.item_question_background: Supervision differences can materially alter dose and adherence.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract whether exercise is supervised/unsupervised and how delivered.

source_page_refs.quote: Describe whether exercises are supervised or unsupervised and how they are delivered

items.item_question: Is adherence measurement and reporting method described?
items.item_question_background: Adherence reporting is needed to interpret intervention exposure.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract how exercise adherence was measured and reported.

source_page_refs.quote: Detailed description of how adherence to exercise is measured and reported

items.item_question_background: Motivation methods affect engagement and pragmatic applicability.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract motivation/behavior-change strategies used to support exercise participation.

items.item_question: Are decision rules for exercise progression described?
items.item_question_background: Progression logic determines treatment intensity trajectory.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract explicit decision rule(s) used to progress exercise.

source_page_refs.quote: Detailed description of the decision rule(s) for determining exercise progression

items.item_question: Is how the exercise program was progressed described?
items.item_question_background: Operational progression details are needed for replication.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract practical progression implementation (timing, thresholds, increments).

source_page_refs.quote: Detailed description of how the exercise program was progressed

items.item_question: Are exercises described in enough detail to enable replication?
items.item_question_background: Replicable exercise descriptions are the core objective of CERT.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract exercise details and supporting materials (photos/illustrations/videos if provided).

source_page_refs.quote: Detailed description of each exercise to enable replication...

items.item_question: Is any home program component described?
items.item_question_background: Home components can contribute materially to total intervention dose.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract home exercise/non-clinic components and instructions.

source_page_refs.quote: Detailed description of any home program component...

items.item_question_background: Co-interventions can confound interpretation of exercise effects.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract non-exercise components (for example education or cognitive elements).

source_page_refs.quote: Describe whether there are any non-exercise components...

items.item_question: Are the type and number of adverse events reported?
items.item_question_background: Safety reporting is essential in exercise interventions.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract adverse-event types, counts, severity, and attribution where available.

source_page_refs.quote: Describe the type and number of adverse events that occurred during exercise

items.item_question: Is the setting where exercises are performed described?
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract intervention setting(s) and key contextual characteristics.

source_page_refs.quote: Describe the setting in which the exercises are performed

items.item_question: Is exercise dosage described in detail (sessions, duration, program duration, etc.)?
items.item_question_background: Dose parameters are necessary for replication and dose-response interpretation.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract repetition/set/session counts, session duration, frequency, intensity, and total program duration.

source_page_refs.quote: Detailed description of the exercise intervention including... repetitions/sets/sessions...

items.item_question: Is it stated whether exercises are generic or tailored to individuals?
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract whether intervention is one-size-fits-all or individualized.

source_page_refs.quote: Describe whether the exercises are generic ... or tailored

items.item_question: If tailored, is how exercises were tailored described in detail?
items.item_question_background: Tailoring rules are needed to reproduce individualized programs.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract tailoring rules, variables used, and implementation details.

source_page_refs.quote: Detailed description of how exercises are tailored to the individual

items.item_question: Is the decision rule for determining starting exercise level described?
items.item_question_background: Starting-level logic influences early dose and comparability.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract criteria used to assign starting level (for example beginner/intermediate/advanced).

source_page_refs.quote: Describe the decision rule for determining the starting level...

items.item_question_background: Planned fidelity methods are required to evaluate intervention integrity.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract planned adherence/fidelity assessment methods and measures.

source_page_refs.quote: Describe how adherence or fidelity to the exercise intervention is assessed/measured

items.item_question: Is actual delivery as planned reported?
items.item_question_background: Actual fidelity determines interpretability of intervention effect estimates.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract results on extent intervention was delivered as planned.

source_page_refs.quote: Describe the extent to which the intervention was delivered as planned

EvidenceExamplesPositive: The exercise intervention is reported using CERT with detailed progression rules, dosage, adherence, and fidelity metrics.
We provide full exercise prescription details, home program components, provider training, and adverse-event reporting.
This randomized exercise trial reports individual tailoring and starting-level decision rules per CERT items.
EvidenceExamplesNegative: The manuscript discusses digital certificates and encrypted communication, not exercise intervention reporting.
This pharmacology trial has no exercise intervention content, progression, or exercise-dose reporting.
The article is a policy commentary without intervention delivery or adherence details.
Matching.Explain: Adds an exercise-specific reporting overlay when manuscripts include exercise interventions and require reproducible reporting of delivery, progression, dosage, tailoring, and fidelity.
